sessions.details: Recorded for Mike Harding's Sound Of The Seventies show on 1970-05-05 and first broadcast on 12th May 1970.
1. Recorded for the Friday Rock Show: 1980-02-20

track_listing.details: LP-Live At The Budokan Vol.2; Eastworld

transcript.text: On 275 and 285. Also in stereo on VHF. This is National Radio 1.
Well, hello there. Very good evening to you. This is Thomas Vance, TV on the radio.
And welcome once again to the Friday Rock Show.
Tonight from the archives, as recorded in the BBC studios during 1970 and 1971,
we rebroadcast some really good stuff by Uriah Heep.
Also, we have the first ever session by Angel Witch, a band from South London.
And we've got classic cuts from Cheap Trick, Atomic Rooster, The Pink Floyd, among others,
and new releases from Pat Metheny, Genesis, Pat Travers, Robin Char, Crocus, and the original Mirrors.
Not forgetting our Friday night connection, tonight guaranteed to titillate.
But this evening we start with the Ian Gillen Band.
...and the track called Clock Strikes Ten from the At The Budokan album.
[Music]
Specially requested by David Gatehouse, who lives in Harrogate.
Specially for loads of his friends, Richard and Mike and Bob and Greg and Paz and Addy,
the two susans and kim and carol and sarah and heather and all his friends at the harrogate
grammar school also wants to say hello to pat and stephen and hugh and martin in wales and the
watford crowd at aberystwyth university and before that you heard something from an import that was
recorded during 1977 by the ian gillan band the album's called live at budokan volume two it's on
the east world label what was the track smoke on the water another especially the stew dixon and
All the DL7th form in Hull.
And also for Paul and Nicola, I think it is,
and Graham C and Kai and my mum and Gareth and Jeremy and Phil
and the rat with the donkey jacket on at Piccadilly Station.
With lots of love from Roland.
He lives in Nantwich in Cheshire.
And I also played it from Jill, Michelle, Jill, Linda and Ruth
for the Pixies, the Gumbies, the Garden Gnomes and the Plastic Elves from Woodies
and all the listeners who are in South Glamorgan.
10.15, here's The First of Four by Angel Witch.
Today's track from a magic album, which is out on ECM on import.
[Music]
[Music]
It's by Pat Metheny.
The album's called American Garage, and the track was Heartland.
And before that, you heard The First of Four, put together and mixed by Tony Wilson.
Angel Witch and Angel of Death.
I'd like to dedicate that set to a gentleman by the name of Peter Pryor,
who at the moment is in the YP Center in Exeter with lots and lots of love,
from Mrs. Kay Glead.
Mrs. Sorry, dear, just married you off.
Miss K. Glead of No. 90 Lime Kiln,
Wotton Bassett, near Swindon and Wiltshire.
Also, I've got to tell a gentleman here
that he's much loved by a girl called Judy from Plymouth,
and that gentleman is Nick Gibson of Southway,
who is 23 on Sunday.
It's 28 minutes past ten.
Hi, this is Mike Rutherford from Genesis.
This Sunday at seven o'clock,
I shall be playing some of my favourite records
and digging into my deep and murky past
and blowing the dust off all the old singles
that have been stored for many years in my attic.
So do join me this Sunday after the Top 40 show at 7 o'clock
on Radio 1, 275, 285, medium wave, and in stereo on VHF.
Star Special, this Sunday.
Playing it tonight from New York, they are Pat Travers Band,
[Music]
[Music]
[Music]
and a track from an album that's coming out shortly on the Polydor label.
The album's called Crash and Burn, and the track was called Born Under a Bad Sign,
and I am ever looking forward to seeing them live on stage.
Him and his band get better all the time, they really do.
They're going on tour shortly with Girl.
And before that, you heard something by Genesis
from their new album called Duke, as if you didn't know.
The track was Man of Our Times.
Their tour starts on Monday down there in the West Country.
And tomorrow on Rock on Saturday between 4 and 7.30,
I'll be having a chat with that ace drummer, stroke singer, Phil Collins.
19 minutes before 11 o'clock.
Now from 1970, the original line-up of Uriah Heep.
Dream Man!
Came on the scene way back in 1970.
[Music]
They really, really got a roasting.
Me, I thought they were great and still are.
That was the original line-up of Uriah Heep out of our archives.
A couple of tracks, Gypsy and Dream Man.
Specially requested by Anthony Rosling of Worksworth in Derbyshire
and also M.C. Davis of Cardiff.
Not forgetting Colin Rooney of Leamington Spa, who has a P.S. on his letter.
He says here, what was the cause of bassist Gary Thane's death?
Well, looking in the archive books this afternoon, it seems he took too many pills.
And that's a warning.
To cut in D, that's Atomic Rooster from a few years ago and tomorrow night,
[Music]
was in Ward 11 of the Darlington Memorial Hospital, Darlington in County Durham.
Mike, I hope you're... Well, I hope you're not there at the moment. I hope you're better.
And before that, we play Jack and Jill, the new single by Robin Trier from, of course, his current album.
And I'd like to dedicate that to a gentleman who says on this card,
P.S. Creep, creep. I'll be 16 on March the 18th.
So please give us a mention.
Also say hello to Guy and Pete and Tom
and all who managed to get Genesis tickets for four quid.
Many thanks.
Martin Warilow of 90 Northgate Close, Kidderminster in Worcestershire
on the 18th.
Happy birthday.
And Angel Witch.
And that's a single by Night Shift,
[Music]
which is out on the Harvest label.
It's called Don't Rush the Good Things.
And I have to own up.
I first heard it on the Peelers program.
Yes, folks, I do listen to John Peel.
those latent masochistic tendencies of mine i suppose and before that you heard something by
angel witch which is called sweet danger which they're going to be recording as a single for emi
an angel which are made up of three people kevin haybourne on the guitar and lead vocals
kevin riddles on bass backing vocals also doubles on the keyboards and dave hogg on the drums
average age about 21 and they've been together for 18 months they're based in and around south
london and to my ears they sound good five minutes after 11 o'clock and it's time for
the friday night connection last week we played you storm troopin by ted nugent from the lp of
the same name kick out the jams by the mc5 from the album of the same name and david bowie's track
panic in detroit by david bowie from the album aladdin say and that track of course was the clue
Ted Nugent, also known as the Madman of Motor City, from Detroit.
MC5, Motor City 5, from Detroit.
The answer, Detroit.
And the right answer was sent in by just about everybody,
but the card that we've chosen is the one from Dave Miller,
of number 10, Quezuel Street, Kings Lynn in Norfolk.
Dave, congratulations. You get the £10 record token.
He also gets a round of applause for putting together this little Friday Night Connection.
It's a catchy little number
Three bits of music
With a theme
Have a listen
[Music]
Put together by Dave Miller
Number 10, Creswell Street, Kingsland and Norfolk
Dave, you well deserve the £10 record token
And while I've got your card in front of me
You want me to mention Bob and the Girls at Bayes Recordium
Hi, Bob and the Girls at Bayes Recordium
now folks if you think you know what the connection is between those three bits of music
could be in the music it could be in the lyrics the album sleeves the titles it could be anywhere
but it's definitely there if you think what you know what it is put it on a postcard and send it
along to friday night connection the friday rock show bbc radio one london w1a4 w w but don't
forget, tomorrow, I beg your pardon, not tomorrow, next week, next Friday, it's our special program
called Flashback 1968, the whole program built around the year of 1968. So therefore, next week's
connection has got to be built around 1968. It could be the music, could be an event, could be
anything, but it must be 1968. 11.15, the crocuses are up. The original Mirrors from the album of
[Music]
the same name. A really good album
that's out on Mercury and that track was called Flying
and that was specially for Gina and Trev
who are motoring toward Nottingham. I look forward
to the Tandori on Wednesday. Prior to
that, the first single out in this country by Crocus
also to be found on their album Metal
Rendezvous. The track was Bedside Radio.
Don't miss
UFO in Concert this week in Rock
on Saturday. One hour
of excellent rock is recorded during their last
solo tour of the United Kingdom. Hear
UFO in Flight in Concert in Rock
on Saturday, 4 to 7.30
this week. And now,
here's a coven of witches
find followed by angel
and angel witch from our
session by them tonight. And before that,
you heard something by Witch Find, a single
that's just come out on wrong delay records.
It's called Give Em Hell.
Talking about that, I've got
a little note here from Peter Phillips, who lives
in Pencoed, Bridgend, in
Midglemorgan. He says, Tom, would
you wish Scotland good luck against England
at Murrayfield? Now,
Peter, with an accent like, I've got, I've got a route for England, and let's face it,
last time we got it was 1957, and I think tomorrow we well deserve to win.
Scotland, I'm sure you play well, but tomorrow it's got to be England's rugby day.
That's a bit of music from an import album called Opera Sauvage, and it's by Vangelis Papathassiou.
[Music]
A total name, it's an import, and it's out on Polydor from France.
Prior to that, Dogs by The Floyd from the album Animals.
especially for all the four white people around the country,
including Chris and Haz and Timbo and Mark
and everybody at the Seaford College in Petworth and Sussex
and Sputt and Dickie, with lots and lots of best wishes from A.P. Wilkins.
And now, from the archives of 1971, Uriah Heep.
Out of the archives, recorded on the 20th of the 10th, 1971,
Uriah Heep and a couple of tracks, What Should Be Done,
and an amazing version, recorded in the BBC studios,
the Better Broadcasting Company studios, of Look at Yourself.
That really was really good to listen to again.
Quick reminder that next week,
the Friday Rock Show is going to be Flashback 68,
the whole programme built around the year of 1968.
If you've got any suggestions,
all you have to do is jot them down on a postcard,
preferably clean ones, thank you very much,
and nice classic tracks to dig out of the library.
Here now, Angel Witch.
It's their single, Living After Midnight,
[Music]
that's Sunday afternoon, the baseball ground
the Radio 1 football team
playing BBC's Radio Nottingham and Derby
will I be there? Afraid not
because I'll be celebrating England's win
at the rugby tomorrow
everybody's an optimist
our engineers tonight were Paul and Paul, thank you gentlemen
I'll see you for rock on Saturday
tomorrow at 4 o'clock
and now here's Mr. Tony Wilson
with a seasonal message
summertime will be with us
on sunday oh matt monroe eat your heart don't forget to put your clocks forward that's saturday
night sunday morning i'm tommy vance in london wherever you are i hope you're happy and smiling
and content i'll see you a rock on saturday at four tomorrow god bless a good night
